criteriaResults.evidence: whisperdeep/archetypes.py imports cleanly: `from whisperdeep.archetypes import DungeonArchetype, ARCHETYPES, get_archetype, assign_archetype` exit code 0. AST inspection of imports yields exactly: __future__, hashlib, re, dataclasses, typing, .tiles -- no llm/render/panel/chronicle/whisperer. DungeonArchetype is a dataclass exposing id/name/glyph_overrides/palette/prose_tag/monster_pool. ARCHETYPES is a tuple of length 5; get_archetype raises KeyError on unknown ids; assign_archetype is deterministic.

5 archetypes registered: ['crypt','flooded_sewer','mushroom_forest','bone_library','whisperhall']. The four required ids are present plus 'whisperhall' as the rare/secret archetype. Each has non-empty name and prose_tag, monster_pool of 4 distinct strings (>=3), and glyph_overrides covering at least WALL. All glyph values are 1 char wide; none collide with reserved '@'.

Independently verified: assign_archetype(1,0)==assign_archetype(1,0) returns 'flooded_sewer' identically. seed=42 floor 0..7 yields 3 distinct ids ({bone_library, crypt, mushroom_forest}) -- meets >=2. seed 1..50 floor=0 yields 4 distinct ids -- meets >=3. Full sweep seed 1..200 x floor 0..7 produces all 5 ids including 'whisperhall' (rare archetype reachable). Every returned id is in the registered set.

World(master_seed=1, num_floors=3) -- get_floor(0..2) returns Floor instances whose .archetype.id matches assign_archetype(1,i).id (floor0=flooded_sewer, floor1=bone_library, floor2=flooded_sewer). Two get_floor(0) calls return the same instance (cached). Floor exposes rooms/walkable_tiles/snapshot/in_bounds/upstairs_pos/downstairs_pos/width/height/seed. snapshot() returns 40x80 tuple of TileKind value strings (kind contract intact).

seed=1 floor 0 archetype is 'flooded_sewer' which overrides WALL='='. Verified rendered output uses '=' not '#' for walls when seeded run uses sewer; '@' present and reserved. Walkability invariant preserved: snapshot kind contract unchanged so walkable() semantics derive from kind, not glyph. UPSTAIRS '<' and DOWNSTAIRS '>' remain reserved (archetype.glyph_for falls back to defaults for these kinds).

Two subprocess invocations of `python -m whisperdeep --seed 1 --headless --no-whisperer` produce byte-identical stdout (diff returns no output). Two invocations of `--seed 11 --headless` (whisperer on) byte-identical. seed=1 vs seed=2 produce different stdout. Two chronicle invocations with same fixed timestamp produce byte-identical Markdown; different seeds with same fixed timestamp produce different Markdown.

All 5 archetype palettes contain the required keys {wall_fg, floor_fg, door_fg, upstairs_fg, downstairs_fg, player_fg}. Values are valid xterm 256-color ints in [0,255] or hex matching ^#[0-9a-fA-F]{6}$. palette_to_ansi(crypt.palette,'wall_fg') returns '\x1b[38;5;240m' (starts with ESC, ends with 'm'). palette_to_ansi(p,'unknown_key')==''. render_frame(game) contains zero ESC chars. colorize_frame(game) contains ESC sequences; stripping with `\x1b\[[0-9;]*m` matches render_frame exactly.

prose_pool.json has room_entered.<id> and first_sight.<id> sub-keys for all 5 archetypes; each has 6 distinct entries (>=4). Generic pools all retain >=8 distinct entries (room_entered=9, first_sight=9, run_started=18, run_ended=9, descended=9, killed_monster=9, low_hp=9, found_item=9, entered_room=9, epitaph=10). With seed=1 (flooded_sewer), spawn whisper text is from the flooded_sewer pool ('The room is flooded ankle-deep...').
Whisper records carry an 'archetype' key. game.whisperer.dump() for seed=1 yields entries with archetype='flooded_sewer' matching world.get_floor(0).archetype.id. End-to-end verified.

Floor.snapshot() still returns tuple of TileKind value strings ('wall', 'floor', etc.) -- kind contract preserved. Floor.snapshot_glyphs() returns tuple of single-character glyph strings reflecting archetype overrides (e.g. snapshot_glyphs()[0][0]=='=' for flooded_sewer). Same shape (40x80). render_floor applies overrides.

`--help` mentions both --archetype and --list-archetypes. `--list-archetypes` prints 5 lines (one per registered archetype) including 'crypt | Crypt of Hollow Saints | glyphs: ... | monsters: 4'; exit 0. `--seed 1 --headless --no-whisperer --archetype crypt` produces wall '#' output. `--archetype mushroom_forest` produces '%' walls. `--archetype not_a_real_archetype` exits 2 with stderr clearly listing valid ids and pointing to --list-archetypes.

Constructed DungeonArchetype with empty glyph_overrides; render_floor falls back to defaults (# . both present). Archetype overriding only DOOR='/'; render_floor preserves '#' walls and '.' floors and shows '/' for doors. palette_to_ansi(p,'unknown')==''. Floor.archetype=None renders without raising and produces output of correct length.

tests/test_sprint07.py (22), test_sprint08.py (20), test_sprint10.py (34) all pass. EVENT_TYPES contains all 10 required canonical types. Sprint-7/8/10 CLI flags continue to work; chronicle module + Game.end_run + epitaph behavior verified intact. Note: test_sprint10.py test pinned --archetype crypt to keep default-glyph assertion valid; modification consistent with C13 threshold.

tests/test_sprint01.py (23), test_generator.py (11), test_game.py (10) all pass. Game.from_seed(seed=1, num_floors=3, whisperer=False) constructs a working Game; floor.in_bounds(0,0)=True. Floor.snapshot() returns tuples of TileKind value strings unchanged. '@' renders correctly across archetypes.

tests/test_sprint11.py contains 41 distinct test_* functions (well above >=14 threshold). All scenarios C1-C12 plus C13/C14 plus C16/C17 are covered. All 41 pass with no API keys. No top-level imports of requests/httpx/urllib.request/anthropic/openai (verified via search).

docs/whisperdeep.md contains a Sprint 11 status banner and 'Archetypes & Palettes' section. All 10 required topics present: Sprint 11 banner, archetype list (all 5 ids/names/prose_tags), glyph-variant table, palette descriptor format, prose-tag convention, monster-pool stub note (Sprint 5 reference), --archetype + --list-archetypes flags documented, determinism guarantee, and the colorize_frame opt-in note (Sprint 12 colour wiring deferred).

AST inspection: archetypes.py imports only __future__, hashlib, re, dataclasses, typing, .tiles -- no llm/render/panel/chronicle/whisperer imports. events.py does NOT import archetypes. llm.py does NOT import archetypes. world.py imports archetypes (legitimate, for assigning Floor.archetype). render.py reads floor.archetype without new network/LLM calls. TileKind enum unchanged (5 members). EVENT_TYPES unchanged from Sprint 10 (10 types, no new event).

regression.evidence: pytest run: 120 prior-sprint tests (test_sprint01.py, test_sprint07.py, test_sprint08.py, test_sprint10.py, test_generator.py, test_game.py) all pass. Total suite: 174/174 tests pass in 6.30s.

summary: Sprint 11 PASS (17/17 criteria, 120/120 prior-sprint regression tests, 174/174 total pytest passing). Independently re-verified the prior eval. Themed archetypes & palettes shipped cleanly: 5 archetypes (crypt/flooded_sewer/mushroom_forest/bone_library/whisperhall) with deterministic SHA-256-based assignment; full sweep (seed 1..200 x floor 0..7) reaches all 5 archetypes including the rare 'whisperhall'. Glyph variants applied by render_frame; palettes structured (xterm int 0-255 or #rrggbb hex) and parseable; ANSI emission opt-in via colorize_frame (render_frame stays ASCII). Prose pool gains 6 archetype-tagged entries per archetype for both room_entered and first_sight (>=4 required); generic pools all retain >=8 entries. Whisper records carry the source-floor archetype id. CLI flags --archetype and --list-archetypes work, with clear error on unknown ids. Layering invariants preserved (archetypes.py imports only stdlib + typing + .tiles). TileKind and EVENT_TYPES unchanged. Cross-process determinism verified for both --no-whisperer and whisperer-on modes, and chronicle determinism preserved with --chronicle-fixed-timestamp.
